metadata.notes: Constraint/prevention batch. Tests what is forbidden, limited, or restricted: targeting rules, frequency caps, condition-based prohibitions, and 'cannot' semantics.

queries.question: What restrictions apply when I choose a target for an effect—can I target undetected creatures or only those that match certain criteria?
queries.expected_answer_summary: Targeting can be difficult or impossible if the creature is undetected by you or doesn't match restrictions on who you can target. Some effects require specific targets.
queries.notes: Constraint: targeting restrictions from core rules.

queries.question: When I'm grabbed, what am I prevented from doing?
queries.expected_answer_summary: Grabbed restricts movement and may prevent or limit other actions; the condition text states what you cannot do while grabbed.

queries.question: If an ability says 'Frequency once per round', can I use it more than once in the same round by any means?
queries.expected_answer_summary: Frequency once per round means you can use the ability only once per round; the limit is per round, not per turn.

queries.question: Can an illusion created by a spell affect the physical world or cause damage beyond what the spell says?
queries.expected_answer_summary: The illusion can cause damage by making the target believe the attacks are real, but it cannot otherwise directly affect the physical world.
queries.notes: Constraint: illusion scope—what it cannot do.

queries.question: When a creature is swallowed by its own shadow (spell failure), can it act or perceive the outside world?
queries.expected_answer_summary: The target is invisible and paralyzed, cannot experience or interact with the outside world, and cannot take actions until the effect ends.

queries.question: Are there restrictions on who I can target with the Aid action or similar ally-only effects?
queries.expected_answer_summary: Aid and similar effects target an ally; restrictions on who can be targeted (e.g. willing ally) are stated in the effect.

queries.question: Does Sure Footing let me ignore the movement restriction from being grabbed?
queries.expected_answer_summary: Sure Footing can counteract clumsy, grabbed, paralyzed, or related conditions—so it can remove or mitigate the grabbed condition's restrictions.
